mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly lower the discount rate for equity valuations, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Rank IC for FEDFUNDS changes is negative over any 3-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns are not significantly positive when rates decline.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 8.5 percent, weak labor market conditions suppress consumer spending expectations and corporate earnings forecasts, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: S&P 500 20-day forward returns are positive in more than 55 percent of observations where UNRATE exceeds 8.5 percent.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real personal consumption expenditures and housing starts signals broad-based economic recovery, which increases corporate revenue expectations and produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The joint positive change in RSAFS and HOUST fails to predict positive 60-day forward returns with a statistically significant coefficient (p > 0.05) in a multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: A contraction in the index of industrial production indicates tightening supply-side capacity and reduced economic activity, which triggers short-term risk-off positioning and negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next week.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are positive in over 60 percent of months where INDPRO declines, or the signal loses predictive power during contraction periods.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate falls below 0.25 percent, the zero lower bound constraint forces the Federal Reserve to deploy quantitative easing, which floods financial markets with liquidity and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are negative in more than 50 percent of observations when FEDFUNDS is below 0.25 percent, or the signal shows no statistical edge over a buy-and-hold baseline.

mechanism_narrative: Rising total nonfarm payrolls combined with a declining unemployment rate confirms a strengthening labor market, which boosts consumer confidence and corporate profit margins, yielding positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The interaction term between PAYEMS growth and UNRATE decline is statistically insignificant (p > 0.10) in predicting 60-day forward returns, or the combined signal produces negative returns in over 55 percent of cases.

mechanism_narrative: Increases in real gross domestic product reflect stronger aggregate demand, which directly improves corporate earnings trajectories and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Quarterly GDPC1 growth fails to predict positive 20-day forward returns with a positive coefficient, or the Rank IC is negative over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When industrial production exceeds 90 percent of its 2007 peak, supply-side capacity utilization approaches full employment, reducing recession fears and triggering short-term risk-on flows that produce positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next week.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are negative in more than 60 percent of observations where INDPRO exceeds 90, or the threshold signal shows no predictive accuracy during expansion phases.
